section.heading: Sense of Congress
section.chapeau: It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.content: it is vital for Congress to provide the Food and Drug Administration with additional resources, authorities, and direction with respect to ensuring the safety of the food supply of the United States;
additional inspectors are required to improve the Food and Drug Administration’s ability to safeguard the food supply of the United States;

because of the increasing volume of international trade in food products the Secretary should make it a priority to enter into agreements with the trading partners of the United States with respect to food safety; and
Congress should work to develop a comprehensive response to the issue of food safety.
